channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US citizen allegedly carrying cannabis detained in Moscow, charged with narcotics smuggling: Russian media
item.description: An American was detained in Russia for allegedly bringing cannabis-laced gummies through customs at a Moscow airport, Russian media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza discover personal lives shattered by Hamas
item.description: RAMAT GAN, Israel (AP) — After 484 days of captivity in Gaza, Keith Siegel had many questions. Was his 97-year-old mother still alive? Which of his neighbors was killed in Hamas' Oct.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German prosecutors investigate possible Islamic extremist motive for Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that injured 39 people appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance into eastern Congo's 2nd major city of Bukavu, residents say
item.description: Residents and community leaders say that Rwanda-backed M23 rebels in eastern Congo have entered the region’s second-largest city of Bukavu, the latest ground gained since a major escalation of their yearslong fighting with government forces.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group have welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis hospitalized for bronchitis treatment, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was hospitalized on Friday as he suffers from an ongoing bout of bronchitis, the Vatican tells Fox News. He is also expected to undergo tests during his stay, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WHCA condemns ‘unacceptable’ escalation as White House bars AP from press conference
item.description: The White House Correspondents’ Association (WHCA) condemned the Trump administration for a second time Thursday following The Associated Press's expulsion from the president’s reporter pool due to an editorial decision.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nurses suspended after viral video threatening to kill Israeli man: Australia Health Department
item.description: Two nurses in Australia were suspended following a viral video showed them threatening to kill Israeli patients.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth ruins of nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica in London
item.description: While exploring the site of a 32-story planned skyscraper in London, archaeologists unearthed the ruins of a nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Armed fighters have raped scores of children in eastern Congo, UNICEF says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The U.N. children’s fund on Thursday accused armed men, likely on both sides of the conflict in eastern Congo, of raping scores of children over the past weeks as rebels expand their footprint and push government forces out.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Spicer: China must ‘significantly de-escalate’ before US can consider reducing military budget
item.description: Former White House press secretary Sean Spicer argued that China must “significantly deescalate” before the U.S. can consider reducing its military budget.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US deports 119 migrants from a variety of nations to Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama has received the first U.S. flight carrying deportees from other nations as the Trump administration takes Panama up on its offer to act as a stopover for expelled migrants, the Central American nation’s president said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Russia-Ukraine moves unnerve Senate GOP
item.description: President Trump’s first major moves toward peace negotiations between Russia and Ukraine are unsettling Republicans in the Senate who want to make sure Russian President Vladimir Putin faces consequences for launching an unprovoked invasion into his European neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mexican president signals possible lawsuit against Google over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Mexico could sue Google over its change of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on its Google Maps interface in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says US 'our main counterpart' in peace talks, sidelining Ukraine
item.description: Russia said Thursday that the United States will be its “main counterpart” in forthcoming peace talks to end its invasion of Ukraine, sidelining Kyiv in the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 4 dead, 26 hurt in Taiwan food court explosion
item.description: Four people were killed and another 26 were injured following a Thursday explosion at the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi department store in Taichung, Taiwan, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's Macron urges Syria's interim government to join a US-led coalition fighting extremists
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron urged Syria's interim government to cooperate with a U.S.-led coalition fighting against extremist groups in that region as he hosted a conference Thursday on the Mideast country's future.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, ABBY SEWELL and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Hamas hostage's mom says daughter was forced to fake her death for a propaganda video
item.description: Orly Gilboa, mother of former Hamas hostage Daniella Gilboa, said her daughter was forced to fake her own death and was covered in debris for a propaganda video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies insist Ukraine and Europe must be in peace talks as Trump touts Putin meeting
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Several NATO allies stressed on Thursday that Ukraine and Europe must not be cut out of any peace negotiations as U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth denied that the United States is betraying the war-ravaged country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches fresh drone attack against Ukraine shortly after Trump-Putin phone call
item.description: Ukraine's air force indicated that there was an overnight drone attack. The news on Thursday came after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO is in disarray after the US announces that its security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In just one speech by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth this week, the most powerful member of NATO has thrown the world’s biggest military alliance into disarray, raising troubling questions about America’s commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation
